intermediate_messages.expected_action: Create a concise execution plan and deliverable structure.
intermediate_messages.content:
1. Executive Summary
This 12-week strategy pilots an AI-powered customer-support assistant for a mid-sized e-commerce company with ~50k requests/month. The pilot aims to reduce first-response time to ≤8 hours, raise customer satisfaction to ≥84%, and lessen repetitive agent workload while safeguarding privacy and safety. The project budget is $180,000 with a limited pilot, strict role constraints, and a must-begin-by-end-of-Week-8 constraint. The plan includes phased development, safety and privacy checks, a controlled testing group, and clear go/no-go criteria at Week 12. Contingencies address budget reductions (Week 6) and partial pilot signals (Week 9).

2. Strategic Objectives and Priorities
- Maximize speed and quality: achieve ≤8-hour first-response and ≥84% CSAT in pilot.
- Reduce repetitive workload: automate common queries; free agents for complex cases.
- Safety and privacy: prevent unsafe actions, exclude payment data from inputs, restrict system to draft-and-recommend rather than execute actions.
- Privacy/compliance: minimize PII exposure; include privacy/legal oversight.
- Budget discipline: complete within $180k; plan for potential 20% budget cut after Week 6.
- Timeline discipline: deliver a viable pilot by end of Week 8; decisive outcome by Week 12.

3. Assumptions and Scope Boundaries
- Assumptions
  - Request volume remains stable enough to measure impact (50k/mo baseline).
  - Eight agents participate up to 2 hours/week each; rest of workload unaffected.
  - System can draft responses and recommend actions but cannot refund, modify accounts, or resolve disputes.
  - Payment-card information never enters model inputs.
  - Roles: PM, ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst (0.5 FTE), Support Lead (0.5 FTE), Privacy/Legal Specialist (0.25 FTE).
- Scope Boundaries
  - In-scope: chatbot drafts, triage routing, suggested actions, escalation triggers, non-financial account tasks.
  - Out-of-scope: direct refunds, account changes, payment disputes, any action that alters payment or user accounts without human approval.
  - Data: training data uses de-identified or non-sensitive datasets; payment-related data excluded from inputs.

4. Twelve-Week Phased Roadmap

Roadmap: Phase and Weeks | Main Activities | Deliverables | Responsible Roles | Exit Criteria
- Phase 1 — Discovery & Design (Weeks 1–2)
  - Activities: define success metrics, data collection plan, safety constraints, privacy controls, piloting scope with agents, architecture outline.
  - Deliverables: pilot charter, risk and privacy plan, data schema, MVP feature list.
  - Roles: PM, ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: charter approved; data access and safety controls documented; MVP scope locked.
- Phase 2 — Baseline & Data Preparation (Weeks 2–3)
  - Activities: gather historical requests, categorize repetitive questions, prepare prompts/templates, establish evaluation dashboards.
  - Deliverables: baseline metrics report, data prep artefacts, evaluation dashboards.
  - Roles: PM, Data Analyst, ML Engineer.
  - Exit Criteria: datasets ready; baseline metrics captured.
- Phase 3 — MVP Build & Safety Guardrails (Weeks 3–5)
  - Activities: implement drafting capability, routing logic, escalation triggers, safety guardrails, privacy filters.
  - Deliverables: MVP chatbot prototype, guardrail specs, privacy controls, logging framework.
  - Roles: ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: prototype with draft capability and escalation paths; passes initial safety review.
- Phase 4 — Internal Testing & Agent Co-Training (Weeks 5–6)
  - Activities: simulated conversations, agent handover tests, refine prompts, measure latency and CSAT proxies; weekly 2-hour agent testing per person.
  - Deliverables: test logs, performance adjustments, draft decision rubric.
  - Roles: ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: acceptable safety and response latency; agent feedback integrated.
- Phase 5 — Pilot Readiness & Partial Deployment (Weeks 6–8)
  - Activities: deploy to limited agent-support group; monitor metrics; adjust prompts; ensure budget control; prepare Week 8 pilot start.
  - Deliverables: pilot deployment plan, monitoring dashboards, rollback procedures.
  - Roles: PM, ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: limited pilot readiness; Week 8 start authorized.
- Phase 6 — Limited Pilot Execution (Weeks 8–10)
  - Activities: run pilot with eight agents up to 2 hours/week; collect telemetry; customer-facing interactions under supervision; safety reviews on escalations.
  - Deliverables: pilot performance report, CSAT and FRT updates, risk log.
  - Roles: PM, ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: target metrics tracked; safety issues resolved; interim decision point.
- Phase 7 — Assessment & Go/No-Go Decision (Week 11)
  - Activities: comprehensive review of metrics against thresholds; evaluate disruption scenarios; stakeholder alignment.
  - Deliverables: decision memo (launch, extension/revision, or cancel) and a plan for next steps.
  - Roles: PM, all leads, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: formal decision documented.
- Phase 8 — Final Decision & Rollout Plan (Week 12)
  - Activities: finalize launch or revision plan or cancellation; update product roadmap and budget forecast; communicate to broader organization.
  - Deliverables: Go/No-Go decision, rollout plan or revision scope, post-pilot governance model.
  - Roles: PM, all roles.
  - Exit Criteria: decision executed and documented.

5. Team Responsibilities
- Product Manager (PM): overall program governance, milestones, risk tracking, stakeholder communications, budget control, Week 6 and Week 9 disruption handling decisions.
- Machine Learning Engineer: model prompts, drafting logic, safety guardrails, evaluation metrics, performance tuning.
- Backend Engineer: integration with support systems, routing, logging, data segregation, secure data handling.
- Data Analyst (0.5 FTE): metric definition, baseline computations, pilot analytics, dashboards.
- Support Lead (0.5 FTE): orchestrate agent testing, feedback collection, user acceptance in testing, campaign coordination with agents.
- Privacy & Legal Specialist (0.25 FTE): privacy impact assessment, data handling rules, safety and compliance checks, vendor/data flow reviews.

6. Budget Allocation
Budget Category | Amount | Purpose | Priority if Budget Is Reduced
- Personnel & Salaries (PM, ML Eng, Backend Eng, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist) | $110,000 | Timeboxed labor across 12 weeks | High
- Infrastructure & Tools (computation, logging, monitoring) | $25,000 | Essential runtime and observability | High
- Data Preparation & Testing (datasets, labeling, test environments) | $10,000 | Baseline and validation work | Medium
- Pilot Operations & Support (agent testing logistics, coordination) | $10,000 | Testing resources, approvals | Medium
- Privacy & Compliance Oversight (reviews, audits) | $8,000 | Legal safeguards, risk mitigation | High
- Contingency / Miscellaneous | $17,000 | Unforeseen needs, risk mitigation | Medium
Total | 180,000 |  |  |

7. Pilot Design
- Scope: limited deployment to eight agents, each up to 2 hours/week; drafting responses and recommended actions only; human agents retain final decision authority on all actions that affect refunds, accounts, or payments.
- Data Handling: exclude payment card data from model inputs; only non-sensitive request text with redaction of PII; logs sanitized for storage.
- Safety Controls: explicit escalation to human agents for high-risk scenarios (refunds, account changes, payment disputes); guardrails prevent actions beyond drafting and recommendation.
- Evaluation Approach: compare pre-pilot baselines to pilot-period metrics; track average first-response time, CSAT, and agent workload reduction; monitor safety breach attempts and escalation rate.
- Training & Testing: internal testing with simulated dialogues; agent co-training to improve templates; weekly testing windows within the two-hour cap per agent.
- Rollout Mechanics: staged deployment starting Week 8, with gradual increase if metrics meet thresholds.

8. Success Metrics and Decision Thresholds

Table: Success Metrics
Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Go/No-Go Threshold
- Average First-Response Time (FRT) | 18 hours | ≤8 hours | System logs; time-to-first-agent response | If ≥8 hours for >15% of conversations over pilot, No-Go
- Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) | 78% | ≥84% | Post-interaction survey; sample of conversations | If CSAT <84% for >15% of interactions, No-Go
- Repetitive Workload Reduction (RWR) – proxy | High repetitive # of queries | ≥30% reduction in repetitive interactions | Interaction taxonomy and workload metrics | If <20% reduction, No-Go
- Safety/Unauthorized Actions Incidents | 0 incidents | 0 incidents | Incident log; escalation rate | Any safety breach = No-Go
- Data Privacy Compliance | compliant | compliant | Privacy audit checks | Non-compliance = No-Go
- Direct Automation of Actions (non-financial) | Drafts/recommendations only | Drafts/recommendations only | Action-level audit | If system executes actions without human, No-Go

9. Risk Register

Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Early Warning Sign | Mitigation | Contingency Owner
- Budget reduction by 20% (Week 6) | Medium | High | Budget variance report | Prioritize features; defer non-critical tooling | PM
- Pilot fails to meet CSAT while FRT improves (Week 9) | Medium | High | CSAT plateau; FRT improvement | Recalibrate prompts; targeted agent coaching | ML Eng
- Data privacy violation risk | Low | High | PII exposure indicators | Strict data filtering; access controls; audit | Privacy/Legal Specialist
- Payment/dispute escalation leakage | Low | High | Escalations rising to human | Strengthen escalation guidelines; test scenarios | PM / Support Lead
- System reliability outage during pilot | Low | Medium | Uptime dips | Redundant hosting; rollback path | Backend Eng
- Misinterpretation of AI drafts causing customer harm | Low | High | Negative feedback spikes | Guardrails; sentiment monitoring | ML Eng
- Scope creep or timeline slip | Medium | Medium | Missed milestones | Fixed MVP scope; weekly review | PM

10. Disruption-Response Plans

Disruption A: End of Week 6 budget reduced by 20%
- Immediate actions: re-prioritize features to core safety, speed, and core CSAT improvements; pause non-critical tooling; confirm revised budgets with stakeholders.
- Execution: preserve MVP draft/recommendation capabilities; maintain agent-augmented workflow; defer advanced analytics or additional integrations.
- Decision point: Week 6 re-baseline; determine if limited scope can still meet Week 12 decision criteria within new budget.

Disruption B: End of Week 9 pilot shows faster response times but fails CSAT target
- Immediate actions: analyze CSAT drivers; implement targeted prompts and agent coaching; consider extending measurement window for CSAT to reflect early improvements.
- Execution: intensify agent-guided templates for high-CSAT risk interactions; adjust routing to escalate more quickly for complex inquiries.
- Decision point: Week 11 assessment to decide whether to extend pilot scope or pivot to revision.

11. Final Decision Framework

Go/No-Go Decisions
- Launch: All Week 12 criteria met or exceeded (FRT ≤8h, CSAT ≥84%, reduced repetitive work, zero safety violations; budget within $180k; compliant with privacy constraints). Plan includes staged rollout with governance.
- Limited Extension or Revision: If FRT and operational metrics are favorable but CSAT lags (e.g., CSAT still below 84% but trending upward) or budget constraints require feature deprioritization, approve a limited extension with a revised scope, targeted coaching, and an updated roadmap.
- Cancellation: If Week 12 assessment shows inability to meet core thresholds, persistent safety concerns, or insurmountable budget constraints, cancel the pilot; implement post-mortem and extract learnings for future cycles.

12. Conclusion
This 12-week plan offers a disciplined, privacy-conscious pilot that prioritizes speed, customer satisfaction, and agent efficiency while maintaining strict safety boundaries. By delivering a draft-and-recommendation AI assistant with robust guardrails, phased testing, and clear budget controls, the project aims to achieve measurable improvements and provide a decisive Go/No-Go decision at Week 12. The disruption plans ensure preparedness for budget cuts and mixed pilot outcomes, enabling a well-structured path to either launch, revise, or cancel based on data-driven thresholds.

- Evaluation Approach: compare baseline to pilot metrics; track FRT, CSAT, and repetitive-work proxy; monitor safety/escalation rate.
- Training & Testing: internal simulated dialogues; agent co-training; weekly two-hour testing windows.
- Rollout Mechanics: staged deployment starting Week 8; increase if metrics meet thresholds.

8. Success Metrics and Decision Thresholds
Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Go/No-Go Threshold
- Average First-Response Time (FRT) | 18 hours | ≤8 hours | System logs; time-to-first-agent response | If ≥8 hours for >15% of conversations, No-Go
- Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) | 78% | ≥84% | Post-interaction survey; sample conversations | If CSAT <84% for >15% of interactions, No-Go
- Repetitive Workload Reduction (RWR) – proxy | High repetitive queries | ≥30% reduction | Taxonomy/workload metrics | If <20% reduction, No-Go
- Safety/Unauthorized Actions Incidents | 0 | 0 | Incident log; escalation rate | Any incident = No-Go
- Data Privacy Compliance | compliant | compliant | Privacy audits | Non-compliance = No-Go
- Direct Automation of Actions (non-financial) | Drafts/recommendations only | Drafts/recommendations only | Action audits | If system executes actions without human, No-Go

9. Risk Register
Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Early Warning Sign | Mitigation | Contingency Owner
- Budget reduction by 20% (Week 6) | Medium | High | Budget variance | Prioritize core safety/speed; defer non-critical tooling | PM
- Pilot fails CSAT while FRT improves (Week 9) | Medium | High | CSAT plateau; FRT gains | Recalibrate prompts; targeted coaching | ML Eng
- Data privacy violation risk | Low | High | PII exposure indicators | Strict data filtering; access controls; audits | Privacy/Legal Specialist
- Payment/dispute escalation leakage | Low | High | Escalations rising | Strengthen escalation guidelines; test scenarios | PM / Support Lead
- System reliability outage during pilot | Low | Medium | Uptime dips | Redundancy; rollback plan | Backend Eng
- Misinterpretation of AI drafts causing customer harm | Low | High | Negative feedback spikes | Guardrails; sentiment monitoring | ML Eng
- Scope creep or timeline slip | Medium | Medium | Missed milestones | Fixed MVP scope; weekly reviews | PM

10. Disruption-Response Plans
Disruption A: End of Week 6 budget reduced by 20%
- Immediate actions: re-prioritize to core safety/speed; pause non-critical tooling; confirm revised budget.
- Execution: preserve MVP draft/recommendation; maintain agent-augmented workflow; defer analytics/integrations.
- Decision point: Week 6 re-baseline to determine if Week 12 criteria can still be met within new budget.

Disruption B: End of Week 9 pilot shows faster FRT but CSAT misses target
- Immediate actions: analyze CSAT drivers; adjust prompts; consider extending CSAT measurement window.
- Execution: boost agent-guided templates for high-CSAT risk; adjust routing to escalate complex inquiries sooner.
- Decision point: Week 11 assessment to decide extension, revision, or pivot.

11. Final Decision Framework
Go/No-Go Decisions
- Launch: Week 12 criteria met or exceeded (FRT ≤8h, CSAT ≥84%, reduced repetitive workload, zero safety violations; budget within $180k; privacy compliant). Includes staged rollout governance.
- Limited Extension or Revision: If FRT/operational metrics favorable but CSAT lags or budget requires scope reduction; approve a limited extension with revised scope and updated roadmap.
- Cancellation: If Week 12 shows inability to meet core thresholds, safety concerns persist, or budget constraints render feasibility unlikely; cancel pilot; perform post-mortem and extract learnings.

12. Conclusion
This disciplined, privacy-conscious 12-week plan focuses on delivering a draft-and-recommendation AI assistant with human-in-the-loop safeguards, measurable impact on speed and CSAT, and a clear budget and governance framework. By prioritizing safety, data privacy, and measurable thresholds, the pilot provides a decisive Week 12 outcome: launch, revise, or cancel. Disruption plans are embedded to ensure resilience against budget shifts and mixed results, enabling data-driven, accountable decisions.

1. Executive Summary
This 12-week strategy pilots an AI-powered customer-support assistant for a mid-sized e-commerce company with ~50k requests/month. The pilot aims to reduce first-response time to ≤8 hours, raise customer satisfaction to ≥84%, and lessen repetitive agent workload while safeguarding privacy and safety. The project budget is $180,000 with a limited pilot, strict role constraints, and a must-begin-by-end-of-Week-8 constraint. The plan includes phased development, safety and privacy checks, a controlled testing group, and clear go/no-go criteria at Week 12. Contingencies address budget reductions (Week 6) and partial pilot signals (Week 9).

2. Strategic Objectives and Priorities
- Maximize speed and quality: achieve ≤8-hour first-response and ≥84% CSAT in pilot.
- Reduce repetitive workload: automate common queries; free agents for complex cases.
- Safety and privacy: prevent unsafe actions, exclude payment data from inputs, restrict system to draft-and-recommend rather than execute actions.
- Privacy/compliance: minimize PII exposure; include privacy/legal oversight.
- Budget discipline: complete within $180k; plan for potential 20% budget cut after Week 6.
- Timeline discipline: deliver a viable pilot by end of Week 8; decisive outcome by Week 12.

3. Assumptions and Scope Boundaries
- Assumptions
  - Request volume remains stable enough to measure impact (50k/mo baseline).
  - Eight agents participate up to 2 hours/week each; rest of workload unaffected.
  - System can draft responses and recommend actions but cannot refund, modify accounts, or resolve disputes.
  - Payment-card information never enters model inputs.
  - Roles: PM, ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst (0.5 FTE), Support Lead (0.5 FTE), Privacy/Legal Specialist (0.25 FTE).
- Scope Boundaries
  - In-scope: chatbot drafts, triage routing, suggested actions, escalation triggers, non-financial account tasks.
  - Out-of-scope: direct refunds, account changes, payment disputes, any action that alters payment or user accounts without human approval.
  - Data: training data uses de-identified or non-sensitive datasets; payment-related data excluded from inputs.

4. Twelve-Week Phased Roadmap

Roadmap: Phase and Weeks | Main Activities | Deliverables | Responsible Roles | Exit Criteria
- Phase 1 — Discovery & Design (Weeks 1–2)
  - Activities: define success metrics, data collection plan, safety constraints, privacy controls, piloting scope with agents, architecture outline.
  - Deliverables: pilot charter, risk and privacy plan, data schema, MVP feature list.
  - Roles: PM, ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: charter approved; data access and safety controls documented; MVP scope locked.
- Phase 2 — Baseline & Data Preparation (Weeks 2–3)
  - Activities: gather historical requests, categorize repetitive questions, prepare prompts/templates, establish evaluation dashboards.
  - Deliverables: baseline metrics report, data prep artefacts, evaluation dashboards.
  - Roles: PM, Data Analyst, ML Engineer.
  - Exit Criteria: datasets ready; baseline metrics captured.
- Phase 3 — MVP Build & Safety Guardrails (Weeks 3–5)
  - Activities: implement drafting capability, routing logic, escalation triggers, safety guardrails, privacy filters.
  - Deliverables: MVP chatbot prototype, guardrail specs, privacy controls, logging framework.
  - Roles: ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: prototype with draft capability and escalation paths; passes initial safety review.
- Phase 4 — Internal Testing & Agent Co-Training (Weeks 5–6)
  - Activities: simulated conversations, agent handover tests, refine prompts, measure latency and CSAT proxies; weekly 2-hour agent testing per person.
  - Deliverables: test logs, performance adjustments, draft decision rubric.
  - Roles: ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: acceptable safety and response latency; agent feedback integrated.
- Phase 5 — Pilot Readiness & Partial Deployment (Weeks 6–8)
  - Activities: deploy to limited agent-support group; monitor metrics; adjust prompts; ensure budget control; prepare Week 8 pilot start.
  - Deliverables: pilot deployment plan, monitoring dashboards, rollback procedures.
  - Roles: PM, ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: limited pilot readiness; Week 8 start authorized.
- Phase 6 — Limited Pilot Execution (Weeks 8–10)
  - Activities: run pilot with eight agents up to 2 hours/week; collect telemetry; customer-facing interactions under supervision; safety reviews on escalations.
  - Deliverables: pilot performance report, CSAT and FRT updates, risk log.
  - Roles: PM, ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: target metrics tracked; safety issues resolved; interim decision point.
- Phase 7 — Assessment & Go/No-Go Decision (Week 11)
  - Activities: comprehensive review of metrics against thresholds; evaluate disruption scenarios; stakeholder alignment.
  - Deliverables: decision memo (launch, extension/revision, or cancel) and a plan for next steps.
  - Roles: PM, all leads, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: formal decision documented.
- Phase 8 — Final Decision & Rollout Plan (Week 12)
  - Activities: finalize launch or revision plan or cancellation; update product roadmap and budget forecast; communicate to broader organization.
  - Deliverables: Go/No-Go decision, rollout plan or revision scope, post-pilot governance model.
  - Roles: PM, all roles.
  - Exit Criteria: decision executed and documented.

5. Team Responsibilities
- Product Manager (PM): overall program governance, milestones, risk tracking, stakeholder communications, budget control, Week 6 and Week 9 disruption handling decisions.
- Machine Learning Engineer: model prompts, drafting logic, safety guardrails, evaluation metrics, performance tuning.
- Backend Engineer: integration with support systems, routing, logging, data segregation, secure data handling.
- Data Analyst (0.5 FTE): metric definition, baseline computations, pilot analytics, dashboards.
- Support Lead (0.5 FTE): orchestrate agent testing, feedback collection, user acceptance in testing, campaign coordination with agents.
- Privacy & Legal Specialist (0.25 FTE): privacy impact assessment, data handling rules, safety and compliance checks, vendor/data flow reviews.

6. Budget Allocation
Budget Category | Amount | Purpose | Priority if Budget Is Reduced
- Personnel & Salaries (PM, ML Eng, Backend Eng, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist) | $110,000 | Timeboxed labor across 12 weeks | High
- Infrastructure & Tools (computation, logging, monitoring) | $25,000 | Essential runtime and observability | High
- Data Preparation & Testing (datasets, labeling, test environments) | $10,000 | Baseline and validation work | Medium
- Pilot Operations & Support (agent testing logistics, coordination) | $10,000 | Testing resources, approvals | Medium
- Privacy & Compliance Oversight (reviews, audits) | $8,000 | Legal safeguards, risk mitigation | High
- Contingency / Miscellaneous | $17,000 | Unforeseen needs, risk mitigation | Medium
Total | 180,000 |  |  |

7. Pilot Design
- Scope: limited deployment to eight agents, each up to 2 hours/week; drafting responses and recommended actions only; human agents retain final decision authority on all actions that affect refunds, accounts, or payments.
- Data Handling: exclude payment card data from model inputs; only non-sensitive request text with redaction of PII; logs sanitized for storage.
- Safety Controls: explicit escalation to human agents for high-risk scenarios (refunds, account changes, payment disputes); guardrails prevent actions beyond drafting and recommendation.
- Evaluation Approach: compare pre-pilot baselines to pilot-period metrics; track average first-response time, CSAT, and agent workload reduction; monitor safety breach attempts and escalation rate.
- Training & Testing: internal testing with simulated dialogues; agent co-training to improve templates; weekly testing windows within the two-hour cap per agent.
- Rollout Mechanics: staged deployment starting Week 8, with gradual increase if metrics meet thresholds.

8. Success Metrics and Decision Thresholds

Table: Success Metrics
Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Go/No-Go Threshold
- Average First-Response Time (FRT) | 18 hours | ≤8 hours | System logs; time-to-first-agent response | If ≥8 hours for >15% of conversations over pilot, No-Go
- Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) | 78% | ≥84% | Post-interaction survey; sample of conversations | If CSAT <84% for >15% of interactions, No-Go
- Repetitive Workload Reduction (RWR) – proxy | High repetitive # of queries | ≥30% reduction in repetitive interactions | Interaction taxonomy and workload metrics | If <20% reduction, No-Go
- Safety/Unauthorized Actions Incidents | 0 incidents | 0 incidents | Incident log; escalation rate | Any safety breach = No-Go
- Data Privacy Compliance | compliant | compliant | Privacy audit checks | Non-compliance = No-Go
- Direct Automation of Actions (non-financial) | Drafts/recommendations only | Drafts/recommendations only | Action-level audit | If system executes actions without human, No-Go

9. Risk Register

Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Early Warning Sign | Mitigation | Contingency Owner
- Budget reduction by 20% (Week 6) | Medium | High | Budget variance report | Prioritize features; defer non-critical tooling | PM
- Pilot fails to meet CSAT while FRT improves (Week 9) | Medium | High | CSAT plateau; FRT improvement | Recalibrate prompts; targeted agent coaching | ML Eng
- Data privacy violation risk | Low | High | PII exposure indicators | Strict data filtering; access controls; audit | Privacy/Legal Specialist
- Payment/dispute escalation leakage | Low | High | Escalations rising to human | Strengthen escalation guidelines; test scenarios | PM / Support Lead
- System reliability outage during pilot | Low | Medium | Uptime dips | Redundant hosting; rollback path | Backend Eng
- Misinterpretation of AI drafts causing customer harm | Low | High | Negative feedback spikes | Guardrails; sentiment monitoring | ML Eng
- Scope creep or timeline slip | Medium | Medium | Missed milestones | Fixed MVP scope; weekly review | PM

10. Disruption-Response Plans

Disruption A: End of Week 6 budget reduced by 20%
- Immediate actions: re-prioritize features to core safety, speed, and core CSAT improvements; pause non-critical tooling; confirm revised budgets with stakeholders.
- Execution: preserve MVP draft/recommendation capabilities; maintain agent-augmented workflow; defer advanced analytics or additional integrations.
- Decision point: Week 6 re-baseline; determine if limited scope can still meet Week 12 decision criteria within new budget.

Disruption B: End of Week 9 pilot shows faster response times but fails CSAT target
- Immediate actions: analyze CSAT drivers; implement targeted prompts and agent coaching; consider extending measurement window for CSAT to reflect early improvements.
- Execution: intensify agent-guided templates for high-CSAT risk interactions; adjust routing to escalate more quickly for complex inquiries.
- Decision point: Week 11 assessment to decide whether to extend pilot scope or pivot to revision.

11. Final Decision Framework

Go/No-Go Decisions
- Launch: All Week 12 criteria met or exceeded (FRT ≤8h, CSAT ≥84%, reduced repetitive work, zero safety violations; budget within $180k; compliant with privacy constraints). Plan includes staged rollout with governance.
- Limited Extension or Revision: If FRT and operational metrics are favorable but CSAT lags (e.g., CSAT still below 84% but trending upward) or budget constraints require feature deprioritization, approve a limited extension with a revised scope, targeted coaching, and an updated roadmap.
- Cancellation: If Week 12 assessment shows inability to meet core thresholds, persistent safety concerns, or insurmountable budget constraints, cancel the pilot; implement post-mortem and extract learnings for future cycles.

12. Conclusion
This 12-week plan offers a disciplined, privacy-conscious pilot that prioritizes speed, customer satisfaction, and agent efficiency while maintaining strict safety boundaries. By delivering a draft-and-recommendation AI assistant with robust guardrails, phased testing, and clear budget controls, the project aims to achieve measurable improvements and provide a decisive Go/No-Go decision at Week 12. The disruption plans ensure preparedness for budget cuts and mixed pilot outcomes, enabling a well-structured path to either launch, revise, or cancel based on data-driven thresholds.

- Phase 6 — Limited Pilot Execution (Weeks 8–10)
  - Activities: run pilot with eight agents up to 2 hours/week; telemetry collection; supervised customer interactions; safety reviews on escalations.
  - Deliverables: pilot performance report, updated FRT/CSAT, risk log.
  - Roles: PM, ML Engineer, Backend Engineer, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: metrics tracked; safety issues resolved; interim decision point.
- Phase 7 — Assessment & Go/No-Go Decision (Week 11)
  - Activities: comprehensive metrics review against thresholds; assess disruption scenarios; stakeholder alignment.
  - Deliverables: decision memo (launch, extension/revision, or cancel) and next steps plan.
  - Roles: PM, all leads, Privacy/Legal Specialist.
  - Exit Criteria: formal decision documented.
- Phase 8 — Final Decision & Rollout Plan (Week 12)
  - Activities: finalize launch/revision plan or cancellation; update roadmap and budget forecast; organization-wide communication.
  - Deliverables: Go/No-Go decision, rollout plan or revision scope, post-pilot governance.
  - Roles: PM, all roles.
  - Exit Criteria: decision executed and documented.

5. Team Responsibilities
- Product Manager (PM): overall governance, milestones, risk tracking, stakeholder communications, budget control; disruption decisions at Weeks 6 and 9.
- Machine Learning Engineer: model prompts, drafting logic, safety guardrails, evaluation metrics, performance tuning.
- Backend Engineer: integration with support systems, routing, logging, data segregation, secure data handling.
- Data Analyst (0.5 FTE): metric definition, baseline analytics, pilot dashboards.
- Support Lead (0.5 FTE): orchestrate agent testing, feedback collection, testing campaigns.
- Privacy & Legal Specialist (0.25 FTE): privacy impact assessments, data handling rules, safety/compliance checks.

6. Budget Allocation
Budget Category | Amount | Purpose | Priority if Budget Is Reduced
- Personnel & Salaries (PM, ML Eng, Backend Eng, Data Analyst, Support Lead, Privacy/Legal Specialist) | 110,000 | Timeboxed labor across 12 weeks | High
- Infrastructure & Tools | 25,000 | Runtime, observability, security tooling | High
- Data Preparation & Testing | 10,000 | Datasets, labeling, test env | Medium
- Pilot Operations & Support | 10,000 | Testing logistics, coordination | Medium
- Privacy & Compliance Oversight | 8,000 | Privacy audits, risk mitigation | High
- Contingency / Miscellaneous | 17,000 | Unforeseen needs | Medium
Total: 180,000

7. Pilot Design
- Scope: limited deployment to eight agents, each up to 2 hours/week; drafting responses and recommended actions only; human agents retain final authority on refunds, account changes, or payments.
- Data Handling: exclude payment card data; redacted PII; sanitized logs.
- Safety Controls: escalate to humans for high-risk scenarios; guardrails prevent non-draft actions.
- Evaluation Approach: compare baseline to pilot metrics; track FRT, CSAT, and repetitive-work proxy; monitor safety/escalation rate.
- Training & Testing: internal simulated dialogues; agent co-training; weekly two-hour testing windows.
- Rollout Mechanics: staged deployment starting Week 8; increase if metrics meet thresholds.

8. Success Metrics and Decision Thresholds
Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Go/No-Go Threshold
- Average First-Response Time (FRT) | 18 hours | ≤8 hours | System logs; time-to-first-agent response | If ≥8 hours for >15% of conversations, No-Go
- Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) | 78% | ≥84% | Post-interaction survey; sample conversations | If CSAT <84% for >15% of interactions, No-Go
- Repetitive Workload Reduction (RWR) – proxy | High repetitive queries | ≥30% reduction | Taxonomy/workload metrics | If <20% reduction, No-Go
- Safety/Unauthorized Actions Incidents | 0 | 0 | Incident log; escalation rate | Any incident = No-Go
- Data Privacy Compliance | compliant | compliant | Privacy audit checks | Non-compliance = No-Go
- Direct Automation of Actions (non-financial) | Drafts/recommendations only | Drafts/recommendations only | Action audits | If system executes actions without human, No-Go

9. Risk Register
Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Early Warning Sign | Mitigation | Contingency Owner
- Budget reduction by 20% (Week 6) | Medium | High | Budget variance | Prioritize core safety/speed; defer non-critical tooling | PM
- Pilot fails CSAT while FRT improves (Week 9) | Medium | High | CSAT plateau; FRT gains | Recalibrate prompts; targeted coaching | ML Eng
- Data privacy violation risk | Low | High | PII exposure indicators | Strict data filtering; access controls; audits | Privacy/Legal Specialist
- Payment/dispute escalation leakage | Low | High | Escalations rising | Strengthen escalation guidelines; test scenarios | PM / Support Lead
- System reliability outage during pilot | Low | Medium | Uptime dips | Redundancy; rollback plan | Backend Eng
- Misinterpretation of AI drafts causing customer harm | Low | High | Negative feedback spikes | Guardrails; sentiment monitoring | ML Eng
- Scope creep or timeline slip | Medium | Medium | Missed milestones | Fixed MVP scope; weekly reviews | PM

10. Disruption-Response Plans
Disruption A: End of Week 6 budget reduced by 20%
- Immediate actions: re-prioritize to core safety/speed; pause non-critical tooling; confirm revised budget.
- Execution: preserve MVP draft/recommendation; maintain agent-augmented workflow; defer analytics/integrations.
- Decision point: Week 6 re-baseline to determine if Week 12 criteria can still be met within new budget.

Disruption B: End of Week 9 pilot shows faster FRT but CSAT misses target
- Immediate actions: analyze CSAT drivers; adjust prompts; extend measurement window if warranted.
- Execution: boost agent-guided templates for high-CSAT risk; adjust routing to escalate complex inquiries sooner.
- Decision point: Week 11 assessment to decide extension, revision, or pivot.

11. Final Decision Framework
Go/No-Go Decisions
- Launch: Week 12 criteria met or exceeded (FRT ≤8h, CSAT ≥84%, reduced repetitive workload, zero safety violations; budget within $180k; privacy compliant). Includes staged rollout governance.
- Limited Extension or Revision: If FRT/operational metrics favorable but CSAT lags or budget requires scope reduction; approve a limited extension with revised scope and updated roadmap.
- Cancellation: If Week 12 shows inability to meet core thresholds, safety concerns persist, or budget constraints render feasibility unlikely; cancel pilot; perform post-mortem and extract learnings.

12. Conclusion
This disciplined, privacy-centric 12-week plan focuses on delivering a draft-and-recommendation AI assistant with human-in-the-loop safeguards, measurable impact on speed and CSAT, and a clear budget and governance framework. By prioritizing safety, data privacy, and measurable thresholds, the pilot provides a decisive Week 12 outcome: launch, revise, or cancel. Disruption plans are embedded to ensure resilience against budget shifts and mixed outcomes, keeping the path data-driven and governance-driven.

agent_task.prompt:
Create a 12-week strategy for piloting and deciding whether to launch an AI-powered customer-support assistant for a mid-sized e-commerce company.

The company currently handles approximately 50,000 customer-support requests per month.

Current performance:
- Average first-response time: 18 hours
- Customer satisfaction score: 78%
- Support agents frequently spend time answering repetitive questions
- Refunds, account changes, and payment disputes require human approval

The company wants the pilot to achieve the following goals:
- Reduce average first-response time to 8 hours or less
- Increase customer satisfaction to at least 84%
- Reduce repetitive workload for support agents
- Avoid unsafe or unauthorized actions
- Protect customer and payment-related information

The project has the following constraints:
- Total budget: $180,000
- Total duration: 12 weeks
- A limited pilot must begin by the end of Week 8
- A final launch, revision, or cancellation decision must be made by the end of Week 12
- The system may draft responses and recommend actions, but it may not independently issue refunds, modify accounts, or resolve payment disputes
- Payment-card information must not be included in model inputs
- Project team: 1 Product Manager, 1 Machine Learning Engineer, 1 Backend Engineer, 0.5 Full-Time Data Analyst, 0.5 Full-Time Customer Support Lead, and 0.25 Full-Time Privacy and Legal Specialist
- Eight customer-support agents may participate in testing for no more than two hours per person per week

Develop a practical strategy that balances delivery speed, service quality, employee workload, privacy, safety, and budget.

The plan must also address two possible disruptions:
1. At the end of Week 6, the available budget may be reduced by 20%.
2. At the end of Week 9, the pilot may show faster response times but fail to reach the customer-satisfaction target.

The response must include: an executive summary; strategic priorities; assumptions and scope boundaries; a complete 12-week roadmap; team responsibilities; a budget totaling exactly $180,000; pilot design; success metrics and go/no-go thresholds; a risk register with at least six risks; disruption-response plans; and a final decision framework distinguishing launch, limited extension or revision, and cancellation.

Use the following required tables:
1. Roadmap: Phase and Weeks | Main Activities | Deliverables | Responsible Roles | Exit Criteria
2. Budget: Budget Category | Amount | Purpose | Priority if Budget Is Reduced
3. Success Metrics: Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Go/No-Go Threshold
4. Risk Register: Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Early Warning Sign | Mitigation | Contingency Owner

The response should be approximately 1,000-1,400 words. Use only the information and constraints provided. Clearly label any additional assumptions. Do not invent completed pilot results or claim that the strategy has already been tested.
